title: /cat and /! bypass ClearMessage::Chat wrapper — inconsistent with main send path
description: Main chat send path (commands.rs ~858): 'let line_bytes = serde_json::to_vec(&ClearMessage::Chat { text: line.clone() }).unwrap()' — wraps in ClearMessage. /cat (~743): 'let line_bytes = text.into_bytes()' — raw bytes, no wrapper. /! (~799): same raw bytes. Recipients try serde_json::from_slice::<ClearMessage>() which fails on raw bytes, falling to the legacy Err(_) arm with String::from_utf8_lossy. This works but creates an invisible split: /cat and /! are stored in history as raw bytes while normal messages store as ClearMessage JSON bytes. Suggested rewrite: wrap all three in ClearMessage::Chat before MLS encrypt, removing the legacy Err(_) arm from the receive path.
